title: carex × acutangula
intro: Carex × acutangula, also known as the sharp-leaved sedge, is a hybrid species of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is a perennial plant with sharp, pointed leaves and a distinctive appearance. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. Carex × acutangula can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
